title: KWh Cost Calculator
description: Industrial-grade calculator for total cost of electrical energy consumption, incorporating demand charges, power factor penalties, and efficiency losses per ISO 50001 and Six Sigma DMAIC standards.

inputs.label: Energy Consumption (kWh)
inputs.businessContext: Total metered kilowatt-hours for the billing period. Used as primary driver for energy cost.

inputs.label: Peak Demand (kW)
inputs.businessContext: Highest 15-minute average demand in the period. Drives demand charges per utility tariff.

inputs.label: Energy Rate ($/kWh)
inputs.businessContext: Blended energy charge from utility bill, including generation and transmission.

inputs.label: Demand Rate ($/kW)
inputs.businessContext: Monthly demand charge per kW of peak demand, varies by utility and region.

inputs.label: Power Factor
inputs.businessContext: Ratio of real power to apparent power. Low PF incurs penalties per utility tariff.

inputs.label: PF Penalty Threshold
inputs.businessContext: Minimum power factor before penalty applies, typically 0.9 per utility standards.

inputs.label: PF Penalty Rate ($/kvar)
inputs.businessContext: Cost per reactive power (kvar) when PF below threshold.

inputs.label: System Efficiency (%)
inputs.businessContext: Overall electrical system efficiency including transformers, cables, and motors. Used for loss adjustment.

inputs.label: Operating Hours per Month
inputs.businessContext: Total hours the facility operates in the billing period. Used for load factor calculation.

inputs.label: Tariff Type
inputs.businessContext: Utility rate structure. Affects how energy and demand charges are calculated.

inputs.label: Include Demand Charge
inputs.businessContext: Toggle to include or exclude demand charges from total cost.

rules.message: Power factor below threshold: penalty applied.
Demand charge included in total cost.
System efficiency less than 100%: energy consumption adjusted upward.

thresholds.message: Energy cost exceeds $50,000. Consider energy audit.
Power factor below 0.85: immediate correction recommended to avoid penalties.
Peak demand exceeds 1000 kW. Consider demand-side management.
System efficiency below 80%: investigate equipment and wiring losses.

formulas.label: Adjusted Energy Consumption (kWh)
formulas.description: Adjusts raw energy consumption for system losses per ISO 50001 energy baseline.

formulas.label: Energy Cost ($)
formulas.description: Total cost of energy consumed, including loss adjustment.

formulas.label: Demand Cost ($)
formulas.description: Demand charge based on peak demand and rate, conditional on user selection.

formulas.label: Power Factor Penalty ($)
formulas.description: Penalty for reactive power when PF below threshold, based on kvar demand.

formulas.label: Load Factor
formulas.description: Ratio of average load to peak load. Indicates demand-side efficiency per WERC guidelines.

formulas.label: Total Cost Before Tax ($)
formulas.description: Sum of all direct electrical costs before taxes or surcharges.

formulas.label: Total Cost with Tax ($)
formulas.description: Total cost including 8% standard industrial tax/surcharge (adjustable per region).

primary.label: Total KWh Cost ($)
primary.description: Final total cost of electrical energy consumption including all charges, penalties, and taxes.

energy_cost.label: Energy Cost
energy_cost.description: Cost of energy consumed (adjusted for losses).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Transformer Losses
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Estimated 2% loss from transformers per IEEE standards.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Cabling Losses
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Estimated 1.5% loss from cable resistance per NEC guidelines.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Motor Inefficiency
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Estimated 3% loss from motor inefficiency per NEMA MG1.

suggestedActions.label: Install Power Factor Correction Capacitors
suggestedActions.description: Reduce PF penalty by installing capacitors to improve power factor above 0.95.

suggestedActions.label: Implement Demand-Side Management
suggestedActions.description: Shift loads to off-peak hours to reduce peak demand and demand charges.

suggestedActions.label: Upgrade to High-Efficiency Equipment
suggestedActions.description: Replace old transformers and motors with IE4/NEMA Premium units to reduce losses.

suggestedActions.label: Conduct ISO 50001 Energy Audit
suggestedActions.description: Identify additional savings opportunities through systematic energy review.
